text: You are unable to remember anything about your past, yourself, or your family (whether mortal or supernatural), though your past might well come back to haunt you.
mechanics: Your origins and the circumstances behind your amnesia are for the Storyteller to determine, and they are encouraged to make it as interesting as possible.
title: Amnesia

text:
It is not enough for you to draw nourishment from the blood of mortals.
You must also consume your victim's organs or other blood-rich tissue.
mechanics:
This will necessitate the deaths of all your victims (unless you are extremely creative),
which might lead to numerous problems with maintaining Humanity (and a low profile).
Characters with this Flaw should additionally purchase the Eat Food Merit.
title: Conspicuous Consumption

text: When you sleep, it is very difficult for you to awaken.
mechanics: +2 difficulty to awaken during the day for any reason.
title: Deep Sleeper

text:
You managed to make it through the Creation Rites or other similarly traumatic experience, but not wholly intact. 
The most insignificant thing can throw you into a different mood or state of mind,
and as such your behavior is extremely unpredictable.
mechanics:
Your Willpower fluctuates. 
At the beginning of each scene, make a Willpower roll.
If you succeed, you may participate in the story as normal.
If you fail, your Willpower score is considered to be 1 for the scene's duration.
You only have one Willpower point to spend.
HR:
If your Willpower is lowered to 1, you gain 1 Willpower back every day thereafter;
 It doesn't automatically replenish to maximum value.
title: Flashbacks

text:
You simply cannot come to grips with the fact that you must drink blood to survive.
You suffer horrible guilt each time you feed and try to avoid doing so as much as possible.
mechanics:
Each time you feed, roll Conscience vs. 7, or else frenzy every time you feed.
You rarely have much blood in your system, leaving you vulnerable to both attacks and hunger-based frenzies.
title: Guilt-Wracked

text:
You have no patience for standing around and waiting.
You want to do things now -- fuck those slowpokes trying to hold you back.
mechanics: Every time you are forced to wait around instead of acting, a Self-Control roll is required to see if you go tearing off on your own instead.
title: Impatient

text: You are affected by the phases of the moon, increasing your chances to frenzy.
mechanics:
Under the crescent moon, difficulties to avoid frenzy increase by one.
Under the half or gibbous moon, difficulties to avoid frenzy rise by two.
Under the full moon, difficulties to avoid frenzy rise by three.
title: Lunacy

text:
You experience horrendous nightmares every time you sleep.
Memories of them haunt you during your waking hours.
mechanics:
Upon awakening, you must make a Willpower roll (difficulty 7) or lose a die on all actions for that night.
A botched Willpower roll indicates that, even when awake, you still believe that you are locked in a nightmare.
title: Nightmares

text:
You have an overpowering fear of something.
Spiders, snakes, crowds, and heights are examples of common phobias.
mechanics:
You must make a Courage roll every time you encounter the object of your fear.
The difficulty of the roll is determined by the Storyteller.
If you fail the roll, you must retreat from the object or situation.
title: Phobia

text: You refuse to hunt a certain class of prey.
mechanics:
If you accidentally feed upon such an individual, you automatically frenzy and must make a roll to prevent Humanity or Path loss (diff 7). 
Witnessing other Kindred feeding on the object of your exclusion might also provoke a frenzy, at the Storyteller's discretion.
Clan Ventrue cannot take this flaw.
title: Prey Exclusion

text: You are easily angered.
mechanics:
+2 difficulty to avoid frenzy.
Clan: Brujah can't take this flaw.
title: Short Fuse

text: You are distinctly ill at ease when dealing with people and try to avoid social situations whenever possible.
mechanics:
+2 diff for all rolls involving social interactions with strangers.
+3 diff involving social interactions if your character becomes the center of attention in a large group.
title: Shy

text: You cannot stand to watch others suffer.
mechanics:
You avoid any situation that involves causing someone physical or emotional pain, unless you make a Willpower roll (diff 8).
You must have a Humanity rating of 7 or above to take this Flaw.
title: Soft-Hearted

text: You have a stammer or other speech impediment that hampers verbal communication.
mechanics:
+2 diff to all die rolls involving verbal communication.
This Flaw must be roleplayed whenever possible.
title: Speech Impediment

text:
You buy heavily into all of the Vampire stories you've read and heard.
You wear a cape or body glitter, speak with an accent, and otherwise act in a cartoonish fashion.
Such behavior is embarrassing in the extreme to other Kindred, who are likely to ostracize or mock you.
mechanics:
-2 to all NPC opinion.
+2 difficulty to Social rolls with other vampires who don't share your habits.
Your threat may increase substantially, making you more likely to stand out to hunters.
title: Stereotype

text: You are extremely territorial, staking out a particular area as your hunting ground and reacting aggressively to trespassers.
mechanics:
If another Vampire enters your territory uninvited, you must make a frenzy roll.
 If you fail, you immediately attack the interloper and continue attacking until the intruder is dead or has left your hunting grounds.
You are reluctant to leave your territory except in desperate circumstances.
title: Territorial

text: The sight of innocence -- of any sort -- arouses in you a terrible bloodlust.
mechanics: Roll Self-Control or else go into hunger frenzy and attack the source of your hunger.
title: Thirst For Innocence

text:
You fail to see the need for the core ideologies of your Sect or Clan, and have gone on record as saying so.
Taking your stand has made you suspect in the eyes of your superiors, and may have attracted the attention of your enemies as well.
mechanics:
-1 opinion modifier to all NPCs.
Clan Tremere cannot take this flaw.
title: Unconvinced

text: You have a score to settle, incurred either during your mortal days or after the Embrace.
mechanics:
You are obsessed with taking your revenge on an individual or group, and it is your overriding priority in any situation where you encounter the object of your revenge.
You may temporarily resist your need for vengeance by spending a Willpower point.
title: Vengeful

text: You are highly susceptible to Dominate and intimidation by others.
mechanics:
Dominate attempts automatically affect you unless the Discipline wielder is of higher Generation.
+2 diff to resist Social abilities such as Intimidation or Leadership, as well as mind-altering spells or magic.
Your Willpower Trait may never rise above 4.
title: Weak-Willed

text: You suffer from an addiction to a substance, which must now be present in the blood you drink.
mechanics:
The substance can be alcohol, nicotine, or hard drugs.
This substance always impairs you in some fashion (see 'Poisons and Drugs,' VTM 20th, p. 301, for particulars).
Without this substance in mortal blood consumed, you automatically frenzy.
Vitae is exempt from this flaw. (House Rule)
title: Addiction

restrictions:
Cannot take flaw 'Bad Sight' & merit 'Acute Senses: Sight'.
Cannot take flaw 'Blind' & flaw 'Bad Sight'.
text: Your sight is defective.
mechanics:
+2 difficulty to any roll involving the use of eyesight.
1 Point: This condition can be corrected with aids (squinting, a hand held over the brow, etc.).
3 Point: This condition is too severe to be corrected.
title: Bad Sight

restrictions:
Cannot take flaw 'Blind' & merit 'Acute Senses: Sight'.
Cannot take flaw 'Blind' & flaw 'One Eye'.
Cannot take flaw 'Blind' & flaw 'Bad Sight'
Cannot take flaw 'Blind' & discipline 'Dominate'.
text: You cannot see.
mechanics:
+2 difficulty to all Dexterity-based rolls. 
Immunity to Dominate rolls needing eye contact (but not Auspex-orientated Dominate rolls). (House Rule) 
Cannot utilize the Dominate discipline at all. (House Rule)
title: Blind

restrictions:
Cannot take flaw 'Hard of Hearing' & flaw 'Deaf'.
Cannot take merit 'Acute Senses: Hearing' & flaw 'Deaf'.
text: You cannot hear.
mechanics:
+3 difficulty to many Perception rolls.
+1 difficulty to some incoming applications of Dominate (case-by-case basis) (House Rule).
title: Deaf

text: You have some kind of deformity -- a misshapen limb, hunchback, or clubfoot, for example -- which affects your physical abilities and interactions with others.
mechanics: The mechanical disadvantage is determined by the deformity chosen on a case-by-case basis.
title: Deformity

text:
Your blood carries a lethal and highly contagious disease. 
In Ancient Rome, humanity (and vampires) have no idea how diseases spread.
mechanics:
Kindred who drink your blood have a 1 in 10 chance of becoming a carrier.
You must spend an extra blood point each night on awakening, or you will begin manifesting symptoms of the disease.
The mechanical disadvantage is determined by the deformity chosen on a case-by-case basis.
title: Disease Carrier

text: A hideous disfigurement makes your appearance disturbing and memorable.
mechanics:
+2 difficulty to all dice rolls relating to social interactions.
You may not have an Appearance rating greater than 2.
title: Disfigured

restrictions:
Sphere: Vampire.
Cannot take flaw 'Dulled Bite' & flaw 'Permanent Fangs'.
text:
For some reason your fangs never developed fully, or they may not have manifested at all.
A number of Caitiff often manifest this Flaw.
mechanics:
When feeding, you need to find some other method of making the blood flow.
Failing that, you must achieve double the normal number of successes in order to make your bite penetrate properly.
title: Dulled Bite

text:
Your flesh does not fully regenerate itself once it is damaged.
While you are able to heal yourself to the point of regaining full functionality, your skin still retains the cuts, tears, bullet holes, and other visible damage that you have incurred.
mechanics: Depending on the nature of the damage, this Flaw will make social dealings exceedingly difficult, and may decrease your Appearance dots over time (even to 0).
title: Flesh Of The Corpse

text:
You have the stereotypical glowing eyes of Vampire legend.
The tradeoffs are many; you must constantly disguise your condition (no, contacts don't cut it).
WARNING: This flaw is a great way to raise threat and for hunters to find & try to kill you.
mechanics:
-1 difficulty on Intimidation rolls when dealing with mortals.
+1 difficulty on all eyesight rolls.
+2 difficulty on Stealth rolls.
title: Glowing Eyes

restrictions:
Cannot take flaw 'Hard of Hearing' & merit 'Acute Senses: Hearing'.
Cannot take flaw 'Hard of Hearing' & flaw 'Deaf'.
text: Your hearing is defective.
mechanics: +2 difficulty to any roll involving the use of hearing.
title: Hard of Hearing

text: You may not automatically lick the wounds of your feeding closed and you have a small chance of spreading potentially fatal disease.
mechanics:
You cannot cover your tracks in hunting.
You have a 1 in 5 chance of causing mortal victims to become seriously ill.
If your prey dies, you have to make a Humanity roll. (House Rule)
title: Infectious Bite

text:
During your Embrace, something went horribly wrong, causing your blood to mutate under the stress of dying and rising again.
A number of Caitiff often manifest this Flaw.
mechanics: All those who you try to embrace die.
title: Infertile Vitae

text:
Your legs are damaged, which prevents you from running or walking easily.
You are forced to walk with a cane or possibly leg braces, and you have a pronounced limp to your stride.
mechanics: Your walking speed is one-quarter that of a normal human, and running is impossible.
title: Lame

text:
You are simply lazy, avoiding anything that requires effort on your part.
Preferring to let others do the hard work, you lounge around.
For any action that requires preparation, there's a good chance you didn't properly prepare.
mechanics: +1 difficulty on spontaneous Physical actions (including combat, unless part of a planned offensive).
title: Lazy

text:
Your physical form was twisted during the Embrace, and now reflects the Beast that rages inside you.
Characters with this Flaw appear to be savage monsters.
mechanics: Your character has an appearance rating of 0.
title: Monstrous

text: You cannot speak.
mechanics: You cannot talk to PCs or NPCs unless everyone concerned purchases a commonly understood sign language (via the Language Merit) or you write down what you wish to say.
title: Mute

restrictions:
Cannot take flaw 'One Eye' & merit 'Acute Senses: Sight'.
Cannot take flaw 'Blind' & flaw 'One Eye'.
text: You have only one eye -- which eye is missing is up to you.
mechanics:
+2 difficulty to all rolls involving eyesight.
+1 additional difficulty on all rolls involving depth perception (including ranged combat).
title: One Eye

restrictions:
Sphere: Vampire. 
Cannot take flaw 'Open Wound' & flaw 'Permanent Wound'.
text: You have one or more wounds that refuse to heal, and which constantly drip blood.
mechanics:
Level One: You lose one extra point of blood every night, and, if the wound is visible, +1 difficulty for social rolls. 
Level Two: As above, and, you rise from sleep at the Wounded health level, though this may be healed by spending blood points.
title: Open Wound

restrictions:
Sphere: Vampire.
Cannot take flaw 'Dulled Bite' & flaw 'Permanent Fangs'.
text:
Your fangs do not retract, making it impossible for you to hide your true nature.
While some mortals may think you've had your teeth filed or are wearing prosthetics, sooner or later you're going to run into someone who knows what you truly are.
mechanics:
Your character has a max appearance rating of 3.
WARNING: This flaw is a great way to raise threat and for hunters to find & try to kill you.
title: Permanent Fangs

text: You suffered injuries during your Embrace which your transformation somehow failed to repair.
mechanics: At the beginning of each night, you rise from sleep at the Wounded health level, though this may be healed by spending blood points.
title: Permanent Wound

text: You are well below average height -- four and a half feet tall or less.
mechanics:
+1 difficulty reaching or manipulating objects designed for normal adult size. (House Rule)
Your running speed is one-half that of an average human.
title: Short

text:
You have difficulty healing wounds.
A number of Caitiff often manifest this Flaw.
mechanics:
It requires two blood points to heal one health level of bashing or lethal damage.
You heal one health level of aggravated damage every five days (plus the usual five blood points and Willpower expenditure).
title: Slow Healing

text:
You exude an odor of dampness and newly turned earth, which no amount of scents or perfumes will cover.
Mortals in your immediate presence become uncomfortable.
mechanics: +1 difficulties to all Social rolls affecting mortals.
title: Smell Of The Grave

text:
Your blood is thin, weak, and does not sustain you well.
A number of Caitiff often manifest this Flaw.
mechanics:
All blood point costs are doubled (e.g., using blood-related Disciplines, buffing physical attributes or healing damage).
Rising in the evening still only takes one blood.
You are unable to create a blood bond.
Efforts to sire other vampires succeed only 1 in 5 times.
You cannot take this flaw unless you are Generation 11 or higher (BG: Generation 0).
title: Thin Blood

text:
You have some sort of repetitive motion that you make in times of stress, and it's a dead giveaway as to your identity.
Examples include a nervous cough, constantly wringing your hands, cracking your knuckles, and so on.
mechanics: It costs one Willpower to refrain from engaging in your tic.
title: Tic/Twitch

text:
To you, silver is as painful and as deadly as the rays of the sun.
The mere touch of silver objects discomforts you.
A number of Caitiff often manifest this Flaw.
mechanics: You suffer aggravated wounds from any silver weapons (bullets, knives, etc.).
title: Vulnerability To Silver

text:
When your sire presented you to the Prince of the city, you flubbed it.
Now you're convinced His Majesty hates you (whether he does or not).
mechanics:
When you are in the presence of the Prince or high officer,
succeed at a Willpower vs 7 roll or mess up in a way Staff chooses.
title: Botched Presentation

text:
You are blood bound to another Vampire.
Your regent may not necessarily treat you badly,
but the fact remains that your will is not your own.
The knowledge gnaws on you, even as you find yourself
lost in devotion to your vampiric master.
mechanics:
The Vampire regent must be an NPC.
Anticipate Staff interference,
potential 'bad things', up to and including 'losing is fun'.
title: Bound

text:
You've done dirty work for someone high up in the
city's hierarchy in the past -- the Sheriff, the Bishop,
or even someone higher. However, instead of granting
you favor, your deeds have made you an embarrassment
or a liability. For the moment, your former employer's
concern is to keep you quiet. In the long term, it's to
get rid of you.
mechanics:
The work, outcome, and the NPC must be clearly stated.
Anticipate Staff interference,
potential 'bad things', up to and including 'losing is fun'.
This flaw is more likely to be 'earned' during play than taken
during CG.
title: Catspaw

text:
One Clan in particular wants you dead. You have offended
the entire Clan, from elders to neonates, and as a
result every member of that bloodline wants your head on
a plate. The effects of the Flaw may manifest as anything
from very public snubs and insults to actual attempts on
your unlife.
mechanics:
-4 opinion on all NPC relation with that clan.
+2 diff on all Social rolls relating to members of the
clan in question.
title: Clan Enmity

text:
You have some sort of secret that, if uncovered,
would be of immense embarrassment to you and would
make you a pariah in the local Kindred community. 
This could be anything from having murdered an elder
to being a member of the Anarchs.
mechanics:
No immediate mechanical disadvantage, however, if the secret is exposed,
expect title challenges, loss of station, or a variety of 'bad things'.
title: Dark Secret

text:
Your sire regards the fact that they Embraced you to
be a titanic mistake, and has let everyone know it. You
are mocked at gatherings, taunted by your peers, and
actively despised by the one who should be giving you
guidance. Any request or petition you make is likely to
be looked down upon by friends of your sire, and your
achievements are likely to be discounted.
mechanics:
No immediate mechanical disadvantage, however, expect a difficult unlife.
This flaw is more likely to be 'earned' during gameplay than started out with.
Clan: Ventrue cannot take this flaw on account that while a sire may feel
that way, they would never publicly risk a dignatas loss by saying they
made a mistake.
title: Disgrace To The Blood

text:
You have an enemy, or perhaps a group of enemies,
who seek to harm you. The power of the enemy depends
on how many points the player wishes to spend.
mechanics:
The power of the enemy depends on points invested.
Anticipate Staff interference,
potential 'bad things', up to and including 'losing is fun'.
This flaw is more likely to be 'earned' during play than taken
during CG.
title: Enemy

text:
You had targeted a mortal for the Embrace, but
someone else got there first. You cannot stand the
humiliation of being cheated of your prize, and fly into a
rage whenever you see the one who got away. This hatred may
lead you into other irrational behaviors, like Embracing enemies of
the neonate, creating unauthorized childer, or even
trying to kill your rival.
mechanics:
+2 diff to avoid frenzy pertaining to this situation.
+1 diff to all Charisma rolls until this situation is resolved.
title: Escaped Target

text:
Someone in power doesn't want you around.
Maybe they want territory you possess, or is jealous of the
attention you're getting from a prize mortal retainer -- 
the details are irrelevant. What does matter is that they have
the power to maneuver you into dangerous situations 'for the good of the sect',
and has no compunctions about doing so.
mechanics:
No immediate mechanical disadvantage, however, expect a difficult unlife.
This flaw is more likely to be 'earned' during gameplay than started out with.
title: Expendable

text:
You once held a title in the city, but failed catastrophically
in your duties. Now you are branded incompetent, excluded from
circles of power and responsibility, and generally ostracized
by those on their way up. Your exclusion may make you a target
for recruitment by the Sect's enemies (or so the whispers run,
making you even more distrusted). Conversely, the consequences
of your error might come back to haunt you.
mechanics:
No mechanical disadvantage, but anticipate Staff interference,
potential 'bad things', up to and including 'losing is fun'.
title: Failure

text:
Another Sect or group of vampires has decided that
you're a target for extermination, and pursues you
relentlessly. On the bright side, the enemies of your
enemy may well wish to help you out, potentially garnering
you allies.
mechanics:
No immediate mechanical disadvantage, however, expect a difficult unlife.
This flaw is more likely to be 'earned' during gameplay than started out with.
title: Hunted Like A Dog

text:
The whole matter has been explained to you, but
you're still not quite sure how things in your Sect work.
Your imperfect understanding of the rules and regulations
of your new existence means that sooner or later,
you're going to make a mistake.
It's only a matter of time.
mechanics:
No immediate mechanical disadvantage, however, expect Staff to mess
with you in a variety of ways, large and small, until this flaw is
bought off.
title: Incomplete Understanding

text:
Your sire was, and perhaps still is, distrusted and disliked
by many of the city's Kindred.
As a result, you are distrusted and disliked as well.
mechanics: -1 opinion with most NPCs.
title: Infamous Sire

text:
Somehow you've drawn the scorn of the local Harpies,
who make you their favorite target.
mechanics:
+2 diff on all Social rolls in Elysium.
+1 diff on all Social rolls anywhere else.
+2 diff to use Intimidation or any Dominate powers
on anyone who has heard the stories mocking you.
title: Laughingstock

text:
Not only are you blood bound, but you are also in
thrall to a Vampire who mistreats you hideously. Perhaps
you are publicly abused or humiliated; perhaps
your master forces you to commit unspeakable acts for
him. In any case, existence under the bond is a never-
ending nightmare, with your regnant serving to conduct
the symphony of malice.
mechanics:
There is no inherent mechanical disadvantage with this flaw.
Anticipate Staff interference,
potential 'bad things', up to and including 'losing is fun'.
title: Loathsome Regnant

text:
You look similar to descriptions of another Kindred,
which causes cases of mistaken identity.
This can cause numerous awkward or even dangerous situations,
especially if you're 'twin' has a terrible reputation,
or is wanted for some crime.
mechanics:
After picking an established NPC (no PC look-alikes), Staff will
make issues for your character, both large and small.
title: Mistaken Identity

text:
You are known to be a snitch, an informer firmly
planted in the pocket of the vampires in charge. Those
on whom you might yet inform loathe you as a result,
feeding you misinformation when they can in an attempt
to discredit you. Given the opportunity, they might do
you mischief.
mechanics:
A significant negative opinion modifier will be given to
anyone you had or will snitch on.
+1 diff on all Social rolls against those who don't agree
with your politics.
title: Narc

text:
You've just arrived in your new city of residence, and
don't know anyone in the place. Existing factions may
try to recruit or eliminate you, while vampires in
positions of authority size you up and take your measure.
Meanwhile, your ignorance of the city's current events,
history, and politics (not to mention the personality
quirks of the vampires already in place) may cause you
to make a serious blunder.
mechanics:
While having no mechanical disadvantages, this flaw is best taken
by players new to Vampire.
title: New Arrival

text:
You're the latest in the city to be Embraced or have
yet to prove yourself to your Sect, and everyone knows
it. That automatically puts you at the bottom of the
social totem pole. Other neonates take every
opportunity to demonstrate your inferiority.
Even if someone else is added to the ranks of the unliving,
you're still regarded as something as a bit of a geek by
your peers -- a distinction that can have dangerous
consequences if bullets start flying.
mechanics: All social-related rolls are at +1 diff when dealing with other neonates.
title: New Kid

text:
Someone you once cared deeply for is now with the enemy.
They still attempt to play on your sympathies 'for old times
sake' while working against you.
mechanics:
Unless you succeed on a contested Manipulation+Expression roll
Against your former friend, you cannot act against them unless the
situation becomes life-threatening.
title: Old Flame

text:
You've got your fingers in too many pies, and people
are starting to notice. You have too many ghouls, too
many retainers, or too much influence, which means
that a lot of people have a vested interest in trimming
back your operations. These enemies take every opportunity
to reduce your power and influence, and if that means
lying, cheating, or killing, so be it. Furthermore,
your enemies block every attempt you make to move
into new areas of control. You're boxed in, and the box
is getting smaller.
mechanics:
There is no inherent mechanical disadvantage with this flaw.
This flaw is much more likely to be 'earned' during play than
taken at CG.
Anticipate Staff interference,
potential 'bad things', up to and including 'losing is fun'.
title: Overextended

text:
The flip side of Escaped Target, two vampires wanted
to gift you with the embrace. One succeeded, one
failed -- and they're not happy about that failure.
You, your sire, or both of you have become the target
of the failed suitor's ire.
mechanics:
Regardless, your persecutor is at +2 diff to refrain from
frenzy in your presence. In addition, they may well be working
actively to discredit or destroy you.
title: Rival Sires

text:
Your sire dislikes you and wishes you ill.
Given the smallest opportunity, they will actively
seek to do you harm. Your sire's allies also work against
you, and many elders resent you.
mechanics:
Negative opinion modifiers and anticipate Staff interference,
potential 'bad things', up to and including 'losing is fun'.
title: Sire's Resentment

text:
You have some sort of intimate connection with a
member of an opposing Sect or inimical Clan. You may
have a lover, a childe, a friend, or a contact working
the other side of the fence, but regardless of politics
you retain a friendly (or more than friendly) relationship
with your putative foe. Your close ties to someone on the
other side would be regarded as treason by your superiors
within the Sect, and if you are discovered, the penalty
would surely be death.
mechanics:
No mechanical disadvantage, but anticipate Staff interference,
potential 'bad things', up to and including 'losing is fun'.
title: Sleeping With The Enemy

text:
Shortly after your Embrace, you volunteered for
some task in order to gain respect and approval from
the vampires around you. Now, you wish you had never
opened your damn mouth! While you are not given
any special credit for performing this duty, you would
lose a lot of respect if you were to stop. The nature
and the details of your duty should be worked out with
Staff in advance. Ideas can range from lending money to
other Kindred or acting as a messenger or possibly
gathering victims for ritae (such as Blood Feasts).
mechanics:
Anticipate Staff interference,
potential 'bad things', up to and including 'losing is fun'.
title: Special Responsibility

text: You radiate palpable evil.
mechanics:
Clergy and devout kine know instinctively that there is
something horribly wrong with you, and will react
accordingly.
title: Beacon Of The Unholy

text:
You believe in the old folklore, and cannot cross running
water unless you are at least 50 feet (15 meters) above it.
Running water is considered to be any body of water at least
two feet (half a meter) wide in any direction and not
completely stagnant.
mechanics: No mechanical disadvantages.
title: Can't Cross Running Water

text:
You actually cast no reflection, just like the vampires
of legend. This can have a detrimental effect when trying
to pass as a human.
mechanics:
Vampires of Clan Lasombra automatically have this flaw
(and you may be mistaken as one if you possess this).
title: Cast No Reflection

text:
A chill wind follows you everywhere you go. While
it may make for dramatic entrances, this effect also
discomforts mortals and marks you as obviously super-
natural.
mechanics: +1 diff on all Social rolls with kine.
title: Cold Breeze

text:
You are the recipient of a supernatural curse. The
strength and pervasiveness of the curse depend upon
how many points you wish to incur.
mechanics:
Specific book examples:
[1 pt]: If you pass on a secret you were entrusted with
your betrayal will come back to harm you in some way.
[2 pt]: You stutter uncontrollably when you try to describe
what you have seen or heard.
[3 pt]: Tools break or malfunction when you try to use them.
[4 pt]: You are doomed to make enemies of those whom you most
love or admire.
[5 pt]: Every one of your accomplishments or triumphs will
eventually become soiled or fail in some way.
title: Cursed

text:
You are doomed to experience Final Death or, worse,
suffer eternal agony. No matter what you do, you cannot
avoid this terrible fate.
mechanics:
At some point during gameplay your Dark Fate will come
upon you. Even more ghastly is the fact that you
occasionally have visions of this fate, and the
malaise these images inspire requires an expenditure
of a temporary Willpower point to avoid, or else you
lose a die from all your actions for the remainder of
the night.
It is up to Staff to determine the exact nature of this fate
and when it will occur.
This is a difficult Flaw to roleplay; ironically, though it
may seem as though it removes all free will, the knowledge
of one's death can be quite liberating.
title: Dark Fate

text:
Everything appears rotted and decayed to you. The
world appears to you as a corpse; mortals look diseased
or skeletal, buildings seem decrepit, and your fellow
Kindred seem to be walking, moldering cadavers.
mechanics:
-2 diff to resist all rolls based on Appearance.
+2 diff to all Perception-based rolls.
+1 diff on all Social-based rolls.
title: Deathsight

text:
Mortals have an unconscious awareness of your undead
nature, which makes them anxious and ill at ease in
your presence.
mechanics: +2 diff to all Social rolls with kine.
title: Eerie Presence

text:
There is no ecstasy in your Embrace -- only terror and
pain. Mortals upon whom you feed struggle and shriek
while you attempt to feed, requiring you to grapple
with them for as long as you wish to take their blood.
mechanics: No mechanical disadvantages; feeding is a lot harder.
title: Grip Of The Damned

text:
You are haunted by an angry and tormented spirit,
most likely one of your first victims. This spirit actively
attempts to hinder you, especially when feeding, and does
its utmost to vent its anguish upon you and anyone in your
presence.
mechanics:
Staff determines the exact nature of the spirit, its powers,
and whether or not it can eventually be laid to rest.
title: Haunted

text: You are even more sensitive to sunlight than other vampires are.
mechanics:
Sunlight causes double damage.
Moonlight causes lethal damage, but it must shine direct.
HR:
Unlike sun damage, moon damage can be prevented by cloth or other
coverings.
title: Light-Sensitive

text:
Buzzing harbingers of decay swirl around you everywhere.
Their constant presence makes it difficult for you to
interact socially and nearly impossible to sneak up
on someone or hide effectively. The buzzing of the
flies inevitably gives you away.
mechanics:
+1 diff on Social rolls.
+2 diff on Stealth rolls.
title: Lord Of The Flies

text: You cannot abide garlic.
mechanics:
The smallest whiff of garlic will drive you from a room
unless you make a successful Willpower roll (difficulty
based on the strength of the odor).
title: Repulsed by Garlic

text:
Plants wither as you approach and die at your touch.
Your touch leeches heat from living beings, as though
you were made of ice.
mechanics:
There is no inherent mechanical disadvantage with this flaw.
Anticipate Staff interference,
potential 'bad things', up to and including 'losing is fun'.
title: Touch Of Frost
